turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've had a persistent cough for about 5 days now, and it just won't go away. It started out dry but has become really productive with yellowish-green sputum.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: It's just been so frustrating because I was hoping it would clear up on its own, but it's only gotten worse. The cough is really exhausting and makes it hard for me to get a good night's sleep. And sometimes when I'm talking or laughing, it gets so bad that it feels like my chest is going to burst – not just the cough itself, but also this wheezing sound that happens afterwards.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: I do get a low-grade fever, it's around 38.2°C (100.8°F), but it's only there intermittently and seems to be worse in the evenings after I've been coughing a lot. It's not like a full-blown fever or anything, but it's just this nagging feeling that my body is fighting something off.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. I've been having night sweats for the past 4 nights. It's like I'm soaking through my pajamas every night, and it doesn't matter if I'm hot or cold in bed - the sweating just happens.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: No, not during the coughing episodes themselves. The sweating seems to be mostly limited to nighttime, and it's like my body is just overloading with heat, even when I'm not feeling particularly hot or cold in bed.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The coughing itself feels like it's centered in my chest and throat area, but the wheezing sound can be a bit more diffuse, sometimes feeling like it's coming from deeper within my lungs.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: I've had some pain in my arms and shoulders when I'm coughing really hard, especially if I've been coughing nonstop for a while. It feels kind of like a strain from exertion, but it's more than that – it's almost like a sharp ache that shoots through my upper body.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, I don't feel any tingling or numbness in my arms during these episodes. It's more of a dull ache or strain that comes and goes with the coughing and wheezing.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, I don't feel any tingling or numbness in my arms during these episodes. It's more of a dull ache or strain that comes and goes with the coughing and wheezing.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling in my ankles, feet, or hands. To be honest, I'm more concerned about this cough not improving and how it's affecting my daily life than any potential swelling right now.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: I don't know about that. To be honest, I'm really focused on getting this cough under control and feeling better, so I haven't really thought about whether my shoes are fitting differently or if there's any puffiness in my legs. That's not something I've noticed or been concerned about.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: Honestly, no, I haven't experienced any stomach issues like nausea or an upset stomach. But to be frank, I have been taking OTC dextromethorphan for the past week with minimal relief, so maybe that's not surprising? The cough just seems to keep going despite trying something to suppress it.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: No, I haven't experienced any nausea or upset stomach. Like I mentioned earlier, I have been taking OTC dextromethorphan for a week with minimal relief, so maybe that's not surprising? The cough just seems to keep going despite trying something to suppress it.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been experiencing a low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that's intermittent and seems to be worse in the evenings after I've been coughing a lot. And like I mentioned earlier, I've also had night sweats for the past 4 nights - it's like I'm soaking through my pajamas every night.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: I have actually been experiencing a decrease in my appetite over the past 5 days. It's not drastic, but I just haven't felt like eating as much as I usually do.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: I'm an elementary school teacher, so my daily routine is pretty set. I work with young children all day, which can be physically demanding, but it's also very rewarding. I try to eat a balanced diet, but lately, with this cough, my appetite hasn't been great, and when I do eat, it's mostly just comfort foods or things that are easy to swallow without too much effort. As for exercise, I don't really have time for anything strenuous during the week, but on weekends, my partner and I like to go for walks around our neighborhood in Portland. We live in an apartment, so we can get some fresh air without having to leave the city.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I'm pretty active, but like I said, my daily routine is mostly set with teaching. During the week, I don't have time for anything strenuous, but on weekends, my partner and I like to go for walks around our neighborhood in Portland. We live in an apartment, so we can get some fresh air without having to leave the city. To be honest, though, with this cough going on, even those short walks have been a bit more challenging than usual – it's harder to catch my breath and I feel like I'm exerting myself more than normal.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: To be honest, with this cough going on, my appetite has really decreased over the past 5 days. I've been trying to eat what I can when I'm hungry, but it's mostly just small portions or comfort foods that are easy to swallow without too much effort. Today, for example, I had a bowl of oatmeal with some fruit and a bit of honey - nothing heavy or rich. I'm just not feeling up to eating as much as usual, which is making me a bit concerned because I know I need to keep my strength up while I try to get over this cough.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma. She's been managing it for years with medication and lifestyle changes, but it's something that's always been a part of our family's health history.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: Yes, my mom has asthma, and I think that might be relevant to what I'm experiencing now. She's managed it well over the years, but I've always been a bit more aware of respiratory issues because of her condition.
